User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the RentiPedia platform, who can be either a landlord or a renter.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

name.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for communication. This field is optional as WhatsApp number is the primary contact.

phoneNumber.description: The WhatsApp-enabled phone number of the user, used for login and direct contact. This is a crucial communication channel for the app.

userType.description: The role of the user on the platform, indicating whether they are a landlord or a renter.

isAdmin.description: Flag indicating if the user has administrative privileges.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the user account was last updated.

RoomListing.title: RoomListing
RoomListing.description: Represents a room or property available for rent, posted by a landlord.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RoomListing entity.

landlordId.description: Reference to the User who owns this listing and is a landlord. (Relationship: User 1:N RoomListing)

title.description: A short, descriptive title for the room listing.

description.description: A detailed description of the room and property.

location.description: The precise physical location or address of the room (Private).

locality.description: The public-facing general area or neighborhood of the room.

latitude.description: The geographical latitude of the room's location for map integration.

longitude.description: The geographical longitude of the room's location for map integration.

nearestCommunicationOptions.description: A list of nearby transportation options or landmarks, e.g., 'Nearest Metro Station: Indiranagar', 'Bus Stop: Wipro Gate'.

monthlyRent.description: The monthly rental cost for the room.

currency.description: The currency in which the monthly rent is specified, e.g., 'INR', 'USD'.

amenities.description: A list of amenities available with the room, such as 'WiFi', 'AC', 'Inverter', 'Water Supply'.

waterSupplyCondition.description: A specific description of the water supply condition, e.g., '24/7 municipal water supply', 'Borewell with occasional restrictions'.

photoUrls.description: An array of URLs pointing to photos of the room listing.

availabilityStatus.description: The current availability status of the room listing.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the room listing was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the room listing was last updated.

SavedSearchRequest.title: SavedSearchRequest
SavedSearchRequest.description: Represents a renter's saved search criteria for finding a room.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SavedSearchRequest entity.

renterId.description: Reference to the User (renter) who created this search request. (Relationship: User 1:N SavedSearchRequest)

searchName.description: A user-defined name for this saved search, e.g., 'My Ideal Room in Koramangala'.

locationFilter.description: The preferred location or area for the room search, e.g., 'Koramangala, Bangalore'.

minRent.description: The minimum acceptable monthly rent for the room.

maxRent.description: The maximum acceptable monthly rent for the room.

requiredAmenities.description: A list of required amenities for the room, e.g., 'WiFi', 'AC'.

keywords.description: Additional keywords for free-text search within room descriptions.

notificationPreference.description: The user's preferred method for receiving notifications about matching room listings.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the search request was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the search request was last updated.

Inquiry.title: Inquiry
Inquiry.description: Represents a direct message or expression of interest from a renter to a landlord about a specific room listing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Inquiry entity.

renterId.description: Reference to the User (renter) who sent the inquiry. (Relationship: User 1:N Inquiry)

listingId.description: Reference to the RoomListing the inquiry is about. (Relationship: RoomListing 1:N Inquiry)

message.description: The content of the message sent by the renter to the landlord.

inquiryDate.description: The timestamp when the inquiry was sent.

status.description: The current status of the inquiry, e.g., 'New', 'Viewed', 'Replied'.

SocialPost.title: SocialPost
SocialPost.description: Represents an AI-generated social media post related to a room listing or a search request, intended for sharing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SocialPost entity.

authorId.description: Reference to the User who initiated or is responsible for this social post (e.g., landlord, or system user if automated). (Relationship: User 1:N SocialPost)

listingId.description: Reference to the RoomListing this post is promoting. This or 'savedSearchRequestId' should be present. (Relationship: RoomListing 1:N SocialPost)

savedSearchRequestId.description: Reference to the SavedSearchRequest this post is based on (e.g., looking for a room for a renter). This or 'listingId' should be present. (Relationship: SavedSearchRequest 1:N SocialPost)

platform.description: The social media platform where the post is intended to be shared, e.g., 'Facebook', 'WhatsApp'.

postContent.description: The AI-generated text content of the social media post.

mediaUrls.description: An array of URLs pointing to images or videos to be included in the social media post.

status.description: The current status of the social post, e.g., 'Draft', 'Scheduled', 'Posted', 'Failed'.

postedAt.description: The timestamp when the social post was successfully published on the platform.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the social post was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the social post was last updated.

SocialChannelConfiguration.title: SocialChannelConfiguration
SocialChannelConfiguration.description: Stores a user's (typically a landlord's) configured social media channels for automated sharing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SocialChannelConfiguration entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this social channel configuration. (Relationship: User 1:N SocialChannelConfiguration)

platform.description: The social media platform for this configuration, e.g., 'Facebook', 'WhatsApp'.

channelType.description: The type of social channel, e.g., 'Group', 'Page', 'DirectMessage'.

channelIdentifier.description: A unique identifier for the specific social channel (e.g., Facebook Group ID, WhatsApp group invite link, Page ID).

enabled.description: Indicates whether this social channel configuration is active for automated sharing.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the social channel configuration was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the social channel configuration was last updated.

LegalAgreement.title: LegalAgreement
LegalAgreement.description: Represents a legal agreement or form generated between a landlord and renter after a deal is made.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LegalAgreement entity.

landlordId.description: Reference to the User (landlord) party to this agreement. (Relationship: User 1:N LegalAgreement)

renterId.description: Reference to the User (renter) party to this agreement. (Relationship: User 1:N LegalAgreement)

listingId.description: Reference to the RoomListing that is the subject of this agreement. (Relationship: RoomListing 1:N LegalAgreement)

agreementName.description: The name or type of the legal agreement, e.g., 'Rental Agreement', 'Lease Deed'.

agreementDocumentUrl.description: URL to the full text or document of the legal agreement.

status.description: The current status of the legal agreement signing process.

landlordSignedAt.description: The timestamp when the landlord signed the agreement.

renterSignedAt.description: The timestamp when the renter signed the agreement.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the legal agreement was created/generated.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the legal agreement was last updated.

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Represents a system-generated notification sent to a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notification entity.

recipientId.description: Reference to the User who is the recipient of this notification. (Relationship: User 1:N Notification)

senderType.description: Indicates who or what initiated the notification, e.g., 'System', 'Landlord', 'Renter'.

relatedListingId.description: Optional reference to a RoomListing if the notification is related to a specific listing. (Relationship: RoomListing 1:N Notification)

relatedSavedSearchRequestId.description: Optional reference to a SavedSearchRequest if the notification is related to a specific search request. (Relationship: SavedSearchRequest 1:N Notification)

relatedInquiryId.description: Optional reference to an Inquiry if the notification is related to a specific inquiry. (Relationship: Inquiry 1:N Notification)

message.description: The content of the notification message.

notificationType.description: The type of event or action that triggered the notification, e.g., 'ListingMatch', 'InquiryReceived', 'LegalAgreementUpdate'.

deliveryMethod.description: The channel through which the notification was or will be delivered, e.g., 'Email', 'WhatsApp', 'InApp'.

status.description: The delivery status of the notification, e.g., 'Pending', 'Sent', 'Failed', 'Read'.

sentAt.description: The timestamp when the notification was successfully sent.

readAt.description: The timestamp when the notification was marked as read by the recipient.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the notification record was created.

definition.description: User profiles, accessible only by the owner (`userId`). Includes basic profile information and `userType` (landlord/renter) for authorization. `userId` in path matches `request.auth.uid`.

Room listings managed by a landlord. This includes drafts, pending listings, and published listings. Only accessible by the specific landlord (`landlordId`). `landlordId` is denormalized within the document for authorization independence.

params.name: landlordId
params.description: The unique identifier of the landlord who owns these listings.

params.name: listingId
params.description: The unique identifier of the specific room listing.

definition.description: Publicly viewable room listings. These documents are copies of listings from `/users/{landlordId}/listings/{listingId}` that have been marked as 'PUBLISHED'. Accessible by any authenticated user for browsing. Includes denormalized 'landlordId' for the owning landlord to manage (update/delete) this public entry directly if needed.

params.name: listingId
params.description: The unique identifier of the specific public room listing.

definition.description: Saved search criteria created by a renter. Accessible only by the specific renter (`renterId`). `renterId` is denormalized within the document for authorization independence.

params.name: renterId
params.description: The unique identifier of the renter who owns this search request.

params.name: requestId
params.description: The unique identifier of the specific saved search request.

definition.description: Direct messages or expressions of interest from renters to landlords about specific room listings. Documents include denormalized `renterId` and `landlordId` (from the related listing) to ensure authorization independence for both parties.

params.name: inquiryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the specific inquiry.

definition.description: AI-generated social media posts for listings or search requests. Primarily managed by the author (`authorId`). Documents include `authorId` for authorization independence.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique identifier of the specific social media post.

definition.description: User-specific configurations for automated social media sharing. Accessible only by the owner (`userId`). `userId` is denormalized within the document for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this social channel configuration.

params.name: configId
params.description: The unique identifier of the specific social channel configuration.

definition.description: Legal agreements between a landlord and a renter. Accessible by both the `landlordId` and `renterId`. Both IDs are denormalized within the document for authorization independence.

params.name: agreementId
params.description: The unique identifier of the specific legal agreement.

definition.description: System-generated notifications specific to a user. Accessible only by the `recipientId`. `recipientId` is denormalized within the document for authorization independence.

params.name: recipientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who is the recipient of this notification.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the specific notification.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure for RentiPedia prioritizes Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation to ensure robust, scalable, and debuggable security rules. Each primary entity is mapped to a collection path that explicitly defines ownership and access patterns.

**Authorization Independence:**
This is achieved by denormalizing authorization-critical fields directly into the documents. For instance:
*   For `Inquiry` documents, both `renterId` (the sender) and `landlordId` (derived from the associated `RoomListing` owner) will be stored within the `inquiries/{inquiryId}` document. This allows both parties to access the inquiry without needing a `get()` call to a parent `RoomListing` or `User` document, enabling simple rules like `allow read: if request.auth.uid == resource.data.renterId || request.auth.uid == resource.data.landlordId;`.
*   Similarly, `LegalAgreement` documents store both `landlordId` and `renterId` to grant independent access to both parties.
*   User-owned data like `SavedSearchRequest`, `SocialChannelConfiguration`, and `Notification` are placed under `/users/{userId}/...` paths, and the `userId` (or `renterId`, `recipientId`) is also denormalized within the document. This redundant storage is key for rules to evaluate permissions without `get()` operations, especially for batch operations or transactions.

**Structural Segregation for QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
This design separates data based on its security posture, which directly addresses the Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs) principle:
*   **Room Listings:** To provide secure `list` operations for publicly available rooms, `RoomListing` data is segregated into two main collections:
    *   `/users/{landlordId}/listings/{listingId}`: This path holds all of a landlord's room listings, including drafts and those pending review. Access is strictly limited to the `landlordId` in the path (which matches `request.auth.uid`). This ensures that `list` operations for a landlord's private listings return only what they are authorized to see.
    *   `/published_room_listings/{listingId}`: This collection stores only `RoomListing` documents that have been officially 'PUBLISHED'. These documents are copies of their counterparts from the landlord's private collection. The explicit 'PUBLISHED' state and dedicated collection mean that any `list` operation on this collection can be publicly readable, making `list` operations secure by construction without needing complex filtering rules on the server side.
*   **Private User Data:** Collections like `/users/{userId}/saved_search_requests` and `/users/{userId}/notifications` are inherently secure due to their path-based ownership. A `list` operation on these collections will always be scoped to the authenticated user, thus enforcing the QAPs principle effectively.
*   **Cross-User Data:** For `inquiries` and `legal_agreements`, which involve two parties, the denormalization of both `renterId` and `landlordId` allows for efficient `list` operations using `where` clauses (e.g., `db.collection('inquiries').where('landlordId', '==', request.auth.uid)`). This satisfies QAPs by ensuring that the results of a query directly reflect the user's authorization.
